core_identity.title: Sin, Cos, and Tan for Larger Angles
core_identity.author: Gerry Docherty

curriculum_alignment.subject: mathematics
curriculum_alignment.subtopic: sin, cos and tan for angles up to 360 degrees (quadrants and sign rules)
curriculum_alignment.seeds_of_change: Use the unit circle to extend sine, cosine and tangent to angles from 0° to 360°, determine the sign of each ratio in every quadrant, and use related acute angles (CAST) to find values and justify reasoning.

verse_ality.driving_question: What changes about sine, cosine, and tangent as we move past 90°, and why do the signs change?

knowing_world.focus: Extend trig ratios beyond acute angles using the unit circle, linking cosθ to x, sinθ to y, and tanθ to sinθ/cosθ, and apply quadrant sign rules for angles up to 360°.
key_concepts: Unit circle definitions (cosθ as x-coordinate, sinθ as y-coordinate)
Sign rules for trig ratios in each quadrant
activities: Classify angles by quadrant and predict the signs of sin, cos and tan.
Explain why signs change using the unit circle coordinates (x and y).
Use the related acute angle to interpret obtuse and reflex angle trig values.
Respond to the prompt: What changes after 90°, and why?
tools: Video: Trig Ratios – Angles Greater Than 90° (canva_embed): https://www.canva.com/design/DAGvM7Yut5o/W9t0Tvyskul92CCbwiPR8Q/watch?embed

human_story.focus: See how larger angles show up in navigation, architecture, and astronomy, where motion and direction pass through all four quadrants and trigonometry supports accurate tracking and design.
justice_prompts: Where might you encounter an angle over 90° in real life? Why might it be important to know its sine, cosine, or tangent?
How does understanding beyond acute angles open up new applications?

expressive_self.focus: Communicate your understanding of quadrant sign rules and larger-angle trig through diagrams, mnemonics, and storytelling, making the concept memorable and shareable.
activities: Draw a CAST diagram and colour-code which trig functions are positive in each quadrant.
Create a mnemonic to remember the sign rules for each quadrant.
Write a short story from the perspective of a point travelling around the unit circle.
Explain: How would you help someone visualise trig ratios for obtuse and reflex angles?

designed_world.focus: Use an interactive unit-circle tool to explore trig values across all quadrants and consider inclusive design: how to communicate sign changes without relying on colour or purely visual cues.
design_challenges: Drag the point around the unit circle and observe how sine, cosine, and tangent values change.
Compare the coordinates of the point to the trig ratios.
Find the related acute angle for obtuse and reflex angles.
Check the signs of each ratio in all four quadrants.
Ethical design: design a trig tool that communicates sign changes without relying on visual colour cues.
tools: Interactive sketch: Trig Ratios Angles Greater Than 90 (canvas_file_embed): https://canvas.instructure.com/courses/12822206/files/314020101/download

regulation_supports: Move like the unit circle: slowly rotate your body through 360°.
Pause at 90°, 180°, 270° and notice how perspective shifts.
Stretch your arms out as radii to feel quadrant positions physically.
wellbeing_prompts: How does moving through angles help you remember the CAST diagram?

pathways.name: Final Task: Around the World in 360° (SDG 4)
pathways.focus: Design a navigation-ready set of examples showing trig values and sign rules across all four quadrants.
products: Four chosen angles (one from each quadrant).
For each angle: sine, cosine, and tangent determined using the unit circle.
Related acute angles identified with an explanation of sign changes using CAST.
Submission format: table, diagram, or short video explanation.
Self/peer assessment using the GCSE Maths Rubric (accuracy and clarity).
criteria: Conceptual Understanding – Correctly links unit circle coordinates to trig ratios and explains sign changes.
Fluency & Accuracy – Correct quadrant identification, related acute angles, and ratio signs/values.
Application to Problems – Connects work to navigation/bearings across quadrants.
Independence & Reflection – Clear communication choices and reflection on accessibility.

required: Basic trig ratios for acute angles (SOH-CAH-TOA)
Understanding of the unit circle idea (radius 1 centred at the origin)
Calculator skills for trig values (degree mode where appropriate)

builds_toward: Exact trig values and special angles
